pattern: A stack that cancels each incoming character against an adjacent-in-the-cycle top reproduces the leftmost-pair removal process in a single pass.
hints: Two letters cancel when they are neighbours on the circular alphabet, so 'a' pairs with 'b' and also with 'z'.
After a cancellation the characters that surrounded the removed pair become adjacent, which is exactly what a stack models when you compare the next character to the top.
Walk the string pushing characters; if the current character is circularly consecutive to the stack top, pop the top instead of pushing. The remaining stack, read bottom to top, is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: wrap-around pair a and z are consecutive
cancellation exposes a new wrap-around pair
repeated letters do not cancel each other

stress.description: random strings over a tiny alphabet so cancellations are frequent
stress.note: including z exercises the circular wrap pairs

stress.description: random strings over the full alphabet
